{{Quote|It's a symbol to him, of his rise from obscurity to power. It's the key to him.|[[Garik Loran]]|<ref name="Iron Fist (novel)">''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]''</ref>}}
'''''Iron Fist''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]], under the command of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Zsinj]]. Originally named '''''Brawl''''', it was renamed by Zsinj after his [[Iron Fist (Victory-class)|first command.]] The ship went on to become the symbol of Zsinj's reign during his breakaway from the Empire, serving as his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].

''Iron Fist'' was employed against both the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by Zsinj throughout the ship's career. It spearheaded attacks at both [[Noquivzor]] and the [[ambush at Alderaan]] against the New Republic and at the [[Battle of Kuat (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Kuat]] against the Empire. Zsinj's flagship, believed destroyed in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Selaggis]], survived to return to Zsinj's [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomiri]] shipyards, where it was ultimately destroyed by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and the [[Hapan Royal Navy]].

==History==
Originally commissioned as ''Brawl'', this vessel was the fourth [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] produced for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

The ship was given to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zsinj]] by [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] upon his promotion to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and promptly rechristened the ''Iron Fist'' in honor of his [[Iron Fist (Victory-class)|original command]], becoming the heart of the reinforced [[Quelii sector/Legends|Queli sector]] fleet Zsinj commanded.

===Flagship of Warlord Zsinj===
The vessel soon became a great thorn in the side of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during Zsinj's bid for power as an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]].<ref name="TNEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> The [[Battle of Corsin (5 ABY)]] saw the ship overwhelm Admiral [[Kalback]]'s attempt to establish a foothold in the Galactic North, winning the world for Zsinj.

In the events surrounding the Fall of Coruscant to the New Republic, Zsinj began to use ''Iron Fist'' more overtly against the Republic, bombarding [[Noquivzor]] after [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] destroyed one of his scouting probes to the system. Though the vessel caused significant damage to the base, the Rogues had already left on a covert assignment to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Wedge's Gamble">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>

''Iron Fist'' would also later intercept a [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] convoy in the [[The Graveyard|Graveyard of Alderaan]], destroying all of the freighters except the ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'' along with an ersatz Rogue Squadron made up of Imperials that were dispatched by Imperial agent [[Kirtan Loor]]. Zsinj would later excuse his destruction of the valuable bacta by claiming that Rogue Squadron had tainted it.<ref name="The Krytos Trap">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref>

===Against the Wraiths===
The destruction of the bacta convoy forced the New Republic to step up its efforts against Zsinj. ''Iron Fist'' became the subject of numerous sabotage attempts and attacks as [[Wraith Squadron]] and the [[Solo Fleet]] attempted to hunt her down.<ref name="Iron Fist (novel)" /> The Wraiths correctly theorised that Zsinj saw ''Iron Fist ''as a status symbol of his rise from relative obscurity to the most powerful warlord in the galaxy, and accurately guessed that as he believed he was the only one capable of operating such ships, he would attempt to acquire another ship like it. The [[Battle of Kuat (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Kuat]] saw the ''Iron Fist ''at the head of Zsinj's attempt to steal another [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Executor-class Star Dreadnought]], the ''[[Razor's Kiss]]. Iron Fist''{{'s}} firepower heavily damaged and possibly destroyed ''[[Mauler]]'' during Zsinj's escape, but the subsequent [[Battle for the Razor's Kiss]] saw the ''Razor's Kiss'' destroyed and ''Iron Fist ''forced to flee after [[Voort saBinring]] destroyed one of its shield generator domes.

Following this setback, Zsinj increasingly saw the conflict as a personal duel between himself and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. The [[Battle of Levian Two]] saw ''Iron Fist ''clash once more with its New Republic counterpart [[Mon Remonda]], failing to destroy it due to the fighter tactics of [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and tactical mistakes by Zsinj. As Zsinj's Empire came under increasing attack from both Solo's fleet and [[Teren Rogriss]]' Imperial anti-Zsinj taskforce, Zsinj realised that his opponents were obsessed with ''Iron Fist''{{'s}} destruction and devised a plan to appear to give them what they wanted while still retaining his flagship. He gathered as much wreckage of the ''Razor's Kiss ''as he could, incorporating it into a decoy vessel called the ''[[Second Death]]''. The ''Second Death'' was set to be destroyed when Zsinj show his enemies a destroyed Star Dreadnought, giving the illusion that the Zsinj's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] had been destroyed.<ref name="Solo Command">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref> In the meantime, Zsinj became exceedingly cautious about risking the ship, refusing to bring her into any [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity wells]] in case he was unable to flee. This led his enemies to collaborate in the [[Battle of Vahaba]], with an Imperial [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] brought in to prevent the ship's escape. The plan failed due to Zsinj sacrificing several of his own ships, but [[Lara Notsil]], a former Wraith determined to bring Zsinj down from the inside, sabotaged the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and broadcast the ship's location to Solo's forces. The subsequent [[Battle of Selaggis]] saw ''Iron Fist ''badly damaged by ''Mon Remonda ''and her starfighter complement, but Zsinj's foresight in creating ''Second Death ''paid off as the ship's [[Orbital nightcloak]] fooled New Republic sensors into losing track of the Star Dreadnought. ''Iron Fist ''fled to hyperspace as ''Second Death ''detonated, with the ''Razor's Kiss ''wreckage and that of her own starfighter screen (sacrificed by Zsinj) deceived Solo into thinking ''Iron Fist ''was finally destroyed.

===Destruction===
''Iron Fist ''would flee to Zsinj's [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir Shipyards]] for repairs, and fate would dictate it soon encountered Solo again. When Zsinj's nemesis, not realising Dathomir was such an integral part of Zsinj's operations, crashed on the planet, he was soon followed by a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] carrying [[Isolder]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], which took advantage of the confusion to pummel the inactive ''Iron Fist ''before escaping. Zsinj reinforced the shipyards with numerous Star Destroyers, and when the [[Hapan]] Battle Fleet returned to rescue Isolder, the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]] erupted. Possibly still damaged from the previous battles, ''Iron Fist ''soon found itself in trouble, besieged by numerous Battle Dragons, and had to call in both other Star Destroyers and starfighter support. With Zsinj distracted by the battle, Solo - flying the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'' - destroyed the top half of ''Iron Fist''{{'s}} forward command module with two [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], killing Zsinj. With her shields gone, she soon fell victim to the [[Hapan]] Battle Fleet and a bombardment of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] that finally finished the huge ship off.<ref name="The Courtship of Princess Leia">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' lists ''Iron Fist''{{'s}} original name as ''Brawler'', while ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' list it as ''Brawl''. ''Cracken's Threat Dossier'' actually lists it as ''The Brawl'' in Zsinj's biographical information, but the definite article was probably added to the proper name in error. Later sources, such as ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', continued to use ''Brawl''.

There are some inconsistencies on the construction site of the ''Brawl'': ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' states that the warship was one of the first four [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] to come out of the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat shipyards]], whereas ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' states that the ''Brawl'' was nearing completion at [[Scarl]] when [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] attacked the rebel base at Laakteen Depot with his new [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']].
